hypothesis: If physics-based constraints (non-negativity and sum-to-one regularization) are integrated directly into the diffusion model's loss function, then the resulting model (PCDiff) will generate composite material compositions with higher physical validity (both Validityreg and Validityfab) and comparable or superior fidelity and diversity (Density/Coverage) compared to unconstrained generative models (CVAE and TabDDPM), because explicitly penalizing violations of domain-specific physical laws during training guides the generative process toward physically realizable material compositions.
